page: Amazon Web Services m6id.2xlarge is a general-purpose virtual machine instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features 8 vCPUs and 4 physical cores based on the x86_64 architecture, utilizing an Intel Xeon 8375C processor running at 3.5 GHz. The instance is equipped with 32.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, translating to 4.0 GB of memory per core. Local storage is provided via a bundled 474 GB NVMe SSD instance store volume, and baseline network bandwidth is rated at 3 Gbps.

Benchmark data positions this instance primarily in the average performance tier across most standard workloads. It achieves a Geekbench single-core score of 1653.0 and a multi-core score of 6086.0. In memory tests, the instance records a read-only small block cached bandwidth of 417.34 GB/sec, which drops to 58.64 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. Cryptographic and compression tasks perform within average ranges, with a Passmark CPU encryption score of 7290.82 and single-threaded gzip compression at 28.17 GB/sec.

Tradeoffs are evident in multi-threaded decompression and machine learning tasks. The instance demonstrates weak performance in multi-threaded bzip3 decompression (32.23 GB/sec) and poor to weak results in LLM inference for medium 7B models, yielding 15.8 tokens/sec for prompt processing and 6.35 tokens/sec for text generation. However, it maintains average throughput for database operations, with a Passmark database score of 4008.66 and 7.86 million Redis SET operations per second. This profile makes the instance suitable for general-purpose applications, static web serving, and workloads requiring moderate local NVMe storage, while it is less optimal for intensive multi-threaded decompression or large language model inference.
description: Amazon Web Services m6id.2xlarge is an x86_64 general-purpose instance featuring 8 vCPUs, 4 physical cores, and 32.0 GB of DDR4 RAM, powered by an Intel Xeon 8375C processor at 3.5 GHz. It includes a bundled 474 GB NVMe SSD local instance store and a baseline network bandwidth of 3 Gbps. Performance benchmarks place the instance in the average tier for most operations, including a Geekbench single-core score of 1653.0 and a multi-core score of 6086.0. While it handles database operations and static web serving reliably, with Redis SET operations reaching 7.86 million per second, it exhibits performance trade-offs in multi-threaded decompression and medium-sized LLM inference. This hardware configuration is suited for general-purpose workloads, local-storage-dependent applications, and standard web serving.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m6id.2xlarge is an x86_64 general-purpose instance with 8 vCPUs, 32 GB DDR4 RAM, and a 474 GB NVMe SSD, delivering average benchmark performance for databases and web serving.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m6id.2xlarge is an x86_64 instance with 8 vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and 474 GB NVMe SSD storage, delivering average general-purpose performance.
tagline: An x86_64 general-purpose instance featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors, DDR4 memory, and integrated local NVMe SSD storage.

32.0 GB of DDR4 system memory and 3 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
